item.title: DTCC processes first live tokenized-security trades; broader rollout for eligible participants planned for October 2026.
US regulators reach GENIUS Act one-year deadline to finalize stablecoin rules, determining which issuers can operate.
EU's MiCA transition period ended July 1; an estimated 80% of registered European crypto providers lost authorization status.
Ostium perpetuals exchange loses $18M after attacker manipulates oracle with future-timestamped price reports.
CLARITY Act requires seven Democratic Senate votes for cloture; House committee held New York field hearing in July.
SEC proposes new rules for crypto broker-dealers and digital assets on alternative trading systems ahead of CLARITY Act vote.
FDIC proposes framework for US banks to issue stablecoins as part of GENIUS Act implementation.
BIS classifies stablecoins as closer to ETFs than money, finding $3.5B inflows now shift three-month Treasury bill yields.
Coinbase applies for US national bank charter, joining Fidelity Digital Assets, Ripple, and Paxos in seeking federal banking licenses.
JPMorgan proposes accepting Bitcoin as eligible repo collateral, estimating the move could unlock $20B in institutional liquidity.
Ethical hackers identify and disclose Aptos blockchain flaw that could have exposed $70B in digital assets before patch was deployed.
BlackRock amends custody agreement with Coinbase to require 12-hour settlement windows for asset withdrawals.
Global tokenized real-world asset market surpasses $43B as DTCC, Apex Group, and The Clearing House expand on-chain services.
